enrichment.short_description: EspoCRM is an open-source CRM web application built in PHP with a JavaScript single-page frontend, offering sales, marketing, and support tools that can be self-hosted for free under AGPLv3 or run on EspoCRM Cloud.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (self-hosted, AGPLv3); EspoCRM Cloud from EUR 12.90 per user per month
enrichment.seo_title: EspoCRM Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: EspoCRM review: an open-source PHP CRM with free self-hosting and paid Cloud plans. See features, pricing tiers, pros, cons, and top alternatives.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary:
EspoCRM is an open-source customer relationship management platform built with a PHP backend and a JavaScript single-page application frontend. It provides the core toolset expected of a modern CRM: contact and account management, sales pipeline and opportunity tracking, lead capture and qualification, email integration, calendar and activity scheduling, support case management, and campaign tools for marketing outreach. What sets EspoCRM apart from most commercial CRM software is that the full application is released as free, self-hostable software, which means organizations can download the source code, install it on their own servers or shared hosting, and run it without paying license fees for the core platform. This positions EspoCRM as a direct open-source alternative to proprietary CRM suites, appealing especially to small and midsize businesses, agencies, and technically capable teams who want to avoid per-seat subscription costs or who need to keep customer data on infrastructure they control.

The company behind EspoCRM is Letrium Ltd, a Ukrainian software company headquartered in Chernivtsi, Ukraine. Letrium was established in 2011, initially working as a CRM customization and development shop, taking on client projects to build and adapt customer-management software for other companies. Around 2013, the founding team, credited as Oleksii Avramenko, Yurii Kuznietsov, and Taras Machyshyn, recognized recurring demand from clients for an affordable, easily customizable CRM platform and decided to build their own product rather than continuing to customize other vendors' systems for every client. After roughly ten months of development, the team released the first version of EspoCRM in 2014. Since then the project has grown through community contributions, translations submitted by users around the world, an active support forum, and a steady release cadence, with the software now used by tens of thousands of organizations globally. The EspoCRM source code is hosted publicly on GitHub, where it has accumulated thousands of stars and where the project accepts community pull requests, maintains fix, master, and stable branches, and requires contributors to sign a contributor license agreement.

Technically, EspoCRM follows a fairly conventional but well-structured web application architecture. The backend is written in PHP, targeting current PHP 8.x releases, and is built around SOLID design principles with strict typing and interfaces, and the codebase is checked against a high PHPStan static-analysis strictness level to catch bugs before release. It supports MySQL, MariaDB, or PostgreSQL as the underlying relational database. The frontend is a single-page application built with JavaScript that communicates with the backend through a REST API, which also makes EspoCRM usable as a general-purpose customizable business application platform rather than a rigid, fixed-schema CRM: administrators can define custom entities, custom fields, custom relationships, and custom layouts without touching code, and developers can extend the platform further through its extension and module system. This extensibility is central to how EspoCRM is packaged commercially. The core application is free, but a set of official extensions, most notably the Advanced Pack, which adds reporting, business process management, and workflow automation, and the Sales Pack, which adds product and pricing catalogs, quotations, invoicing, purchasing, and inventory management, are sold as paid add-ons for self-hosted installs. Additional official extensions cover project management, VoIP integrations with providers like 3CX, Twilio, Asterisk, Starface, and Binotel, a meeting-scheduling tool for booking appointment times online, and integrations with Zoom, Google Calendar and Gmail, Outlook, MailChimp, and Stripe.

For organizations that do not want to manage their own servers, Letrium also operates EspoCRM Cloud, a hosted version of the same software. As listed on the official pricing pages, EspoCRM Cloud is sold in three tiers billed per user per month. The Basic plan is priced at EUR 12.90 per user per month, requires a minimum of three users, includes 3GB of file storage per user, and supports up to 100,000 records. The Enterprise plan is priced at EUR 22.00 per user per month, requires a minimum of five users, includes 10GB of storage per user, and supports up to 10,000,000 records, with the option to purchase additional record capacity. The Ultimate plan is priced at EUR 59.00 per user per month, requires a minimum of ten users, includes 400GB of total storage, supports an unlimited number of records, and adds a dedicated IP address, a private server, and a custom domain name. Basic and Enterprise plans are billed on six-month or annual terms, while Ultimate is positioned as a pay-as-you-go offering. A key differentiator of the Cloud plans is that Letrium bundles every official extension, including the Advanced Pack, Sales Pack, VoIP integration, and the various calendar and payment integrations, into all three tiers at no extra charge, so Cloud customers avoid the piecemeal extension pricing that self-hosted users face. All Cloud tiers include SSL encryption, API access, and support described as available 12 hours a day, five days a week, along with daily backups retained for seven days. Because cloud admin accounts are not given direct extension-installation access for security reasons, any extension changes on hosted accounts are handled by the Letrium support team on request.

EspoCRM's target users are primarily small and midsize businesses, sales teams, and service or support organizations that need a flexible, customizable CRM without the cost structure of large enterprise CRM vendors. Because the platform can be run entirely on infrastructure the customer controls, it also appeals to organizations with data-residency or compliance requirements, agencies that need to deploy and white-label CRM instances for multiple clients, and developers who want an extensible application framework rather than a closed system. In the competitive landscape, EspoCRM sits alongside other open-source CRM projects such as SuiteCRM and Vtiger, both of which similarly offer free self-hosted editions with paid hosted or enterprise tiers, as well as larger commercial CRM platforms such as HubSpot CRM, Zoho CRM, and SugarCRM. Compared with HubSpot CRM and Zoho CRM, EspoCRM's main differentiators are the ability to self-host for free, full access to the source code, and the absence of per-contact or per-feature pricing walls found in many SaaS CRM products, though it trades that for a smaller out-of-the-box ecosystem of pre-built integrations and a support model that leans on community forums for self-hosted users rather than a large dedicated customer-success organization. Compared with SugarCRM, which also has open-source roots but has moved toward an enterprise-only commercial model over the years, EspoCRM has kept a genuinely free and actively maintained community edition alongside its paid Cloud and extension offerings.

Notable facts about EspoCRM include that its source code is released under the GNU Affero General Public License version 3 (AGPLv3), a copyleft license that is stricter than the standard GPL because it also covers software accessed over a network, which is a meaningful consideration for companies that might want to modify and redistribute or resell a hosted version of the platform. The project reports that its user base spans tens of thousands of organizations worldwide, and its GitHub repository shows continuous release activity, with the maintainers shipping regular version updates and accepting community-contributed translations through a dedicated localization platform. There is no first-party official EspoCRM mobile application for iOS or Android; instead, independent third-party developers have published companion apps, such as YouCRM for EspoCRM and ObiCRMApp, that connect to an EspoCRM instance through its REST API. Overall, EspoCRM occupies a durable niche in the CRM market as a mature, actively developed, code-transparent alternative for organizations that value ownership of their CRM infrastructure and are willing to trade some out-of-the-box polish and turnkey support for lower cost and greater customization freedom.

The project is developed by Letrium Ltd, a software company founded in 2011 in Chernivtsi, Ukraine. Letrium began as a CRM customization service provider before building its own product, releasing the first version of EspoCRM in 2014 after roughly ten months of development.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features

The platform's extension system adds reporting, business process management and workflow automation through the Advanced Pack, and quoting, invoicing, purchasing, and inventory management through the Sales Pack, along with integrations for VoIP providers, Google, Outlook, Zoom, MailChimp, and Stripe.

All Cloud tiers bundle every official extension at no extra cost, unlike the self-hosted edition.

pros: Free, self-hosted core edition released under the AGPLv3 open-source license, with full access to the source code.
Highly customizable data model, allowing new entities, fields, and layouts to be created without writing code.
EspoCRM Cloud bundles all official extensions, including the Advanced Pack and Sales Pack, into every plan at no extra charge.
Active open-source community with a public GitHub repository, community translations, and an ongoing release cadence.
cons: Self-hosted deployments require server administration skills and infrastructure, and paid extensions are sold separately from the free core.
No official first-party mobile application for iOS or Android; only third-party companion apps are available.
Cloud plans carry minimum user requirements, which can raise the effective cost for very small teams.
Smaller out-of-the-box ecosystem of pre-built integrations compared with larger commercial CRM platforms like HubSpot CRM or Zoho CRM.

faqs.question: Is EspoCRM free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes. The core EspoCRM application can be downloaded and self-hosted for free under the AGPLv3 open-source license. Paid options exist for official extensions on self-hosted installs and for the hosted EspoCRM Cloud service.

faqs.question: Who develops EspoCRM?
faqs.answer: EspoCRM is developed by Letrium Ltd, a software company founded in 2011 and headquartered in Chernivtsi, Ukraine, which released the first version of EspoCRM in 2014.

faqs.question: What does EspoCRM Cloud cost?
faqs.answer: EspoCRM Cloud has three tiers billed per user per month: Basic at EUR 12.90 with a three-user minimum, Enterprise at EUR 22.00 with a five-user minimum, and Ultimate at EUR 59.00 with a ten-user minimum. All tiers include every official extension.

faqs.question: What programming language is EspoCRM built with?
faqs.answer: EspoCRM's backend is written in PHP and follows SOLID design principles with static typing, while its frontend is a JavaScript single-page application that communicates with the backend through a REST API.

faqs.question: How does EspoCRM compare to SuiteCRM or Vtiger?
faqs.answer: EspoCRM, SuiteCRM, and Vtiger are all open-source CRM platforms offering free self-hosted editions alongside paid hosted or enterprise tiers. They differ in their extension ecosystems, licensing terms, and customization frameworks, so the right choice depends on specific feature and support needs.

faqs.question: Does EspoCRM have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: EspoCRM does not publish an official first-party mobile app, but its REST API supports third-party companion applications such as YouCRM for EspoCRM and ObiCRMApp on iOS and Android.
